name: 4 Sum
category: Solve Problems on Arrays [Easy -> Medium -> Hard]
statement:
Given an integer array nums and an integer target. Return all quadruplets [nums[a], nums[b], nums[c], nums[d]] such that:


  • a, b, c, d are all distinct valid indices of nums.


  • nums[a] + nums[b] + nums[c] + nums[d] == target.


Notice that the solution set must not contain duplicate quadruplets. One element can be a part of multiple quadruplets. The output and the quadruplets can be returned in any order.

examples.explanation: No quadruplets are present which add upto 9

nowYourTurn.input: nums = [1, 1, 3, 4, -3], target = 5 (Give answer with the output and quadruplets sorted in ascending order)
